name: Cavazos v. Smith
question: Did the Ninth Circuit exceed its authority under the deferential standard for habeas corpus review by granting relief for insufficient evidence based on its acceptance of the cause-of-death testimony of defense experts over the contrary opinion of prosecution experts?
description: A case in which the Court held that evidence is sufficient to support a conviction as long as the essential elements of the crime are found to be beyond a reasonable doubt when viewed by a reasonable person.
